result.markdown: --- title: "Signac: scRNA + scATAC Integration" subtitle: "Link chromatin accessibility to gene expression using Signac" categories: [Multi-omics, Signac, scATAC-seq, scRNA-seq] --- Multi-omics · Chapter 02 ## Overview [Signac](https://stuartlab.org/signac/) extends Seurat to handle chromatin accessibility data. When paired with matched scRNA-seq (e.g., 10x Multiome), it enables joint analysis — linking open chromatin regions to gene expression at single-cell resolution. **This chapter covers:** 1. Creating a Signac object from a 10x Multiome dataset 2. ATAC-seq QC within Signac 3. LSI dimensionality reduction 4. Joint WNN embedding with matched RNA 5. Linking peaks to genes --- ## Setup ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} library(Seurat) library(Signac) library(EnsDb.Hsapiens.v86) library(BSgenome.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg38) library(ggplot2) library(patchwork) ``` ::: --- ## Load 10x Multiome Data ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} # Read Cell Ranger ARC output counts <- Read10X_h5("data/raw/pbmc_multiome/filtered_feature_bc_matrix.h5") # Separate RNA and ATAC matrices rna_counts <- counts$`Gene Expression` atac_counts <- counts$Peaks # Load fragment file metadata fragpath <- "data/raw/pbmc_multiome/atac_fragments.tsv.gz" ``` ::: --- ## Create Seurat + Signac Object ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} # Genome annotation annotation <- GetGRangesFromEnsDb(ensdb = EnsDb.Hsapiens.v86) seqlevels(annotation) <- paste0("chr", seqlevels(annotation)) # Create chromatin assay atac_assay <- CreateChromatinAssay( counts = atac_counts, sep = c(":", "-"), fragments = fragpath, annotation = annotation ) # Build Seurat object with both modalities seu <- CreateSeuratObject(counts = rna_counts, assay = "RNA") seu[["ATAC"]] <- atac_assay seu ``` ::: --- ## ATAC QC ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} DefaultAssay(seu) <- "ATAC" seu <- NucleosomeSignal(seu) seu <- TSSEnrichment(seu, fast = FALSE) # Filter seu <- subset( seu, subset = nCount_ATAC > 1000 & nCount_ATAC < 100000 & nCount_RNA > 200 & nCount_RNA < 25000 & nucleosome_signal < 2 & TSS.enrichment > 1 ) ``` ::: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} VlnPlot( seu, features = c("nCount_ATAC", "TSS.enrichment", "nucleosome_signal"), ncol = 3, pt.size = 0 ) ``` ::: --- ## Process Each Modality ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} DefaultAssay(seu) <- "RNA" seu <- SCTransform(seu, verbose = FALSE) seu <- RunPCA(seu, npcs = 30) ``` ::: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} DefaultAssay(seu) <- "ATAC" seu <- RunTFIDF(seu) seu <- FindTopFeatures(seu, min.cutoff = "q0") seu <- RunSVD(seu) # LSI ``` ::: --- ## Joint WNN Embedding ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} seu <- FindMultiModalNeighbors( seu, reduction.list = list("pca", "lsi"), dims.list = list(1:30, 2:50), # exclude LSI component 1 (correlated with depth) modality.weight.name = "RNA.weight" ) seu <- RunUMAP( seu, nn.name = "weighted.nn", reduction.name = "wnn.umap", reduction.key = "wnnUMAP_" ) seu <- FindClusters(seu, graph.name = "wsnn", resolution = 0.8) ``` ::: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} p1 <- DimPlot(seu, reduction = "wnn.umap", label = TRUE) + NoLegend() p2 <- FeaturePlot(seu, features = "RNA.weight", reduction = "wnn.umap") p1 | p2 ``` ::: --- ## Link Peaks to Genes ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} DefaultAssay(seu) <- "ATAC" seu <- LinkPeaks( seu, peak.assay = "ATAC", expression.assay = "SCT", genes.use = VariableFeatures(seu, assay = "SCT") ) ``` ::: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} CoveragePlot( seu, region = "MS4A1", features = "MS4A1", expression.assay = "SCT", extend.upstream = 500, extend.downstream = 10000 ) ``` ::: --- ## Save ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} saveRDS(seu, "data/processed/seu_multiome_signac.rds") ``` ::: --- ## Session Info ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} sessionInfo() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` R version 4.5.2 (2025-10-31) Platform: x86_64-pc-linux-gnu Running under: Ubuntu 24.04.3 LTS Matrix products: default BLAS: /usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/blas/libblas.so.3.12.0 LAPACK: /usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/lapack/liblapack.so.3.12.0
